skill.name: swarm-orchestration
skill.description: Guides multi-agent swarm orchestration with agentic-flow using topology choices, task modes, shared memory, hooks, and resilience patterns.
skill.summary: Coordinate agentic-flow swarms for parallel, pipeline, and adaptive multi-agent workflows.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: The skill is a markdown-only guide, and no prompt injection, credential access, network exfiltration, or bundled executable code was found. The real risk is that the guide instructs users to run external npx agentic-flow commands, while the weak-crypto and reconnaissance alerts are false positives from ordinary documentation text and hook names. Publish with an external-command warning and advise users to verify the agentic-flow package before running examples.

medium_findings.title: External Command Examples Require Package Trust
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE with reduced severity. The quick start and hooks sections instruct users to run npx agentic-flow commands. The skill does not execute these commands itself, but users or agents following the instructions would run code from an external npm package.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are fenced bash examples with explicit npx agentic-flow commands. Risk is medium because execution is user-initiated documentation guidance, not hidden bundled code.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fence Command Alerts Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for most external command detections. The static analyzer treated markdown backticks and TypeScript examples as shell execution, but the file is documentation. Only the bash examples at the beginning and hooks section represent actionable external command guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains only SKILL.md markdown and fenced examples. Line review confirms the non-bash locations are topology, task, memory, monitoring, or TypeScript snippets, not executable package code.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The reported lines contain descriptive metadata, worker role names, pipeline stages, and a pre-task hook command. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, insecure random generation, or cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of each cited line found no cryptographic operation. The alerts appear to be keyword collisions in ordinary orchestration documentation.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The reported lines show post-task synchronization and session restore hook examples. No evidence found of host enumeration, credential discovery, process listing, or environment probing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited commands are agentic-flow hook examples with fixed task and session identifiers. They do not collect system information or inspect local resources.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Facing npx Execution Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation includes npx commands for swarm initialization, agent spawning, task orchestration, hooks, and session restore. Users should verify the external package source and version before executing these examples.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern is explicit and repeated in bash examples. The risk depends on the external package supply chain rather than malicious code in this skill.

content.value_statement: Complex AI work can stall when agents duplicate effort or lose shared context. This skill gives practical agentic-flow patterns for coordinated swarms, task routing, memory sharing, and recovery.
actual_capabilities: Explains mesh, hierarchical, and adaptive swarm topology patterns.
Shows how to start agentic-flow swarms and spawn specialized agents.
Documents parallel, pipeline, and adaptive task orchestration modes.
Describes shared memory coordination for schemas, models, and task context.
Covers load balancing, retry policies, fallback assignment, and metrics.
Provides hook examples for pre-task, post-task, and session restore flows.
limitations: Requires agentic-flow v1.5.11 or later and Node.js 18 or later.
Provides guidance and examples, not a bundled orchestration runtime.
External npx commands should be verified before execution.
Referenced docs paths are not included in this skill package.

use_cases.title: Coordinate feature delivery
use_cases.description: Assign design, implementation, testing, and review work to specialized agents with shared task context.

use_cases.title: Standardize agent workflows
use_cases.description: Define repeatable swarm patterns for teams that use agentic-flow across development and automation tasks.

use_cases.title: Run parallel experiments
use_cases.description: Use adaptive orchestration, shared memory, and metrics to coordinate concurrent agent research tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Start a small swarm
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a simple task
prompt_templates.prompt: Use swarm orchestration to plan a three-agent mesh for a small API task. Include coder, tester, and reviewer roles.

prompt_templates.title: Create a delivery pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a hierarchical swarm pipeline for a feature. Include architect, coder, tester, and reviewer stages with clear handoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Add shared memory
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a swarm workflow that stores an API schema in shared memory and has each agent retrieve it before work starts.

prompt_templates.title: Tune resilient orchestration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an adaptive swarm plan with load balancing, retries, fallback reassignment, metrics, and a one-hour execution limit.

output_examples.input: Plan a parallel API build with three agents.
output: Mesh topology selected for equal peer coordination.
Coder, tester, and reviewer roles assigned.
Shared API schema memory identified as the coordination point.
Parallel execution recommended with a five-minute timeout.

output_examples.input: Design a resilient workflow for a production API task.
output: Adaptive topology selected for changing task complexity.
Retry and fallback reassignment added for agent failures.
Load balancing based on queue length and resource metrics.
Progress tracking defined through swarm metrics.

best_practices: Start with two or three agents before increasing swarm size.
Use shared memory for schemas, plans, decisions, and constraints.
Set explicit timeouts, metrics, and fallback behavior for every workflow.
anti_patterns: Do not run external commands before verifying package source and version.
Do not add agents when the task has no clear role boundaries.
Do not rely on adaptive orchestration without monitoring progress and failures.

faq.question: What does this skill help coordinate?
faq.answer: It helps coordinate agentic-flow swarms, including topology choice, task assignment, shared memory, hooks, and resiliency patterns.

faq.question: Does the skill execute commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It is a markdown guide with command examples. Users choose whether to run the external agentic-flow commands.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should I verify before using the examples?
faq.answer: Verify the agentic-flow package, expected version, local permissions, and workspace state before running npx commands.

faq.question: Can it manage agents without agentic-flow?
faq.answer: No. The examples and patterns assume agentic-flow v1.5.11 or later is available.

faq.question: When should I use adaptive topology?
faq.answer: Use adaptive topology when task complexity, workload, or agent availability may change during execution.
